name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America offers payday, installment, and title loans in-store and online, with same-day cash up to $1,000 and title loans up to $25,000.
description_long:
Advance America is a nationally recognized, fully accredited consumer lending company operating thousands of retail locations across the United States. The Jacksonville location at 1020-14 N. Edgewood Ave. serves the Commonwealth Village neighborhood on the city's west side, positioned between I-95 and I-295 just north of I-10. The company caters to consumers who need fast access to cash and may not qualify for traditional bank financing. With over 200,000 Google reviews and a 4.9 out of 5 rating drawn from 125,401 customer reviews, Advance America has built a substantial retail footprint in the short-term lending market.

The store offers three core loan products. Payday Loans range from $100 to $500 and are designed as two-to-four-week bridges between paychecks, available both online and in-store. Installment Loans go up to $1,000 and allow repayment across multiple scheduled payments, giving borrowers more flexibility than a lump-sum payday loan. Title Loans — offered online in amounts from $2,000 to $25,000 through a partnership with LoanCenter — use a borrower's paid-off vehicle as collateral. The store also provides Western Union money transfer services. All loan types can be applied for online or in person, and phone pre-qualification is available.

What distinguishes this location from competitors is its bilingual staffing: Spanish-speaking associates are available in-store, which is a meaningful differentiator in a market with a significant Spanish-speaking population. The location also offers a referral rewards program. To apply, borrowers need a government-issued ID, proof of income, a checking account, and a Social Security Number or ITIN — a relatively low documentation bar compared to bank products. Store hours run Monday through Saturday, with Sunday closures.

Honestly, Advance America operates in a segment of consumer finance known for very high APR costs — payday loans in particular routinely carry triple-digit annualized rates, though specific rates are not disclosed on this store page. Title loans carry repossession risk if borrowers default, since the vehicle title is held as collateral. These products are designed for short-term emergencies, not ongoing cash flow problems. Borrowers who use them repeatedly can find themselves in a debt cycle. That said, for consumers with no credit or limited banking access who genuinely need fast cash, Advance America provides a regulated, accredited option with transparent requirements and a strong customer service track record.
pros: 4.9/5 star rating based on 125,401 verified customer reviews
200,000+ Google reviews — unusually high volume indicating widespread use
Online and in-store application options for all loan types
Phone pre-qualification available before visiting a branch
Title loans up to $25,000 for vehicle owners needing larger amounts
Western Union money transfers available alongside loan products
cons: Payday loans capped at $500 — insufficient for larger emergencies
Specific APR and fee disclosures are not shown on this store page
Title loans require a fully paid-off vehicle in the borrower's name — excludes most borrowers
Title loan default risk includes vehicle repossession via LoanCenter partnership
Closed Sundays — limits same-day access on weekends for in-store applications
best_for: Workers needing a small cash bridge ($100-$500) before their next paycheck
Vehicle owners with a paid-off car who need $2,000-$25,000 quickly
Spanish-speaking borrowers in Jacksonville who prefer bilingual service
Consumers with limited credit history who cannot access traditional bank loans
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for consumers who need fast, small-dollar cash — particularly those with thin or damaged credit who cannot access bank loans or credit cards. The main caveat is cost: payday and title loans in this segment typically carry very high APRs, and repeated use can create a debt cycle rather than resolve an underlying financial shortfall.
services: Payday Loans — $100 to $500, online or in-store
Installment Loans — $100 to $1,000, multi-payment repayment, online or in-store
Title Loans — $2,000 to $25,000 online, vehicle collateral via LoanCenter

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
